name: Nontrivial System
description: Definition of a nontrivial discrete system with state-dependent transitions, state changes, and non-constant output.

elements.text: The system Z is nontrivial if and only if conditions (i), (ii), and (iii) are satisfied.
(i) there exist x1 ∈ SZ, x2 ∈ SZ, and p ∈ IZ, such that NZ(x1, p) ≠ NZ(x2, p) (the transition is state-dependent).
(ii) there exist x ∈ SZ and p ∈ IZ such that NZ(x, p) ≠ x (the state is not constant under all transitions).
(iii) #RNG(RZ) > 1 (the readout function is not constant, having a range size greater than 1).
Z is trivial if Z is not nontrivial.
